title: Loop Aggression Tuning Analysis

paradox_evidence.interpretation: PARADOX CONFIRMED: aggression ratio 2.2x vs expected 18x proportional; suspend rate 41.3% even at glucose>200

summary.caveat: Selection bias likely: high aggression at low glucose correlates with recently-high-now-falling trajectories; low aggression at high glucose may reflect already-treated or meal-related scenarios
summary.interpretation: High aggression optimal at glucose<190, low/mid optimal above — BUT confounded by glucose trajectory

proposed_reduction_rule.rule: IF glucose < 185 AND falling > 1.5 mg/dL/5min → reduce aggression

rule1_hypo_prevention.interpretation: Dosing at glucose<120 & falling>2: hypo rate 0.273 vs not-dosing 0.390 (LOWER by 0.117)

rule2_hyper_treatment.condition: glucose > 200 AND no SMB in prior 30min AND cob < 10

conclusions: PARADOX CONFIRMED: aggression ratio 2.2x vs expected 18x proportional; suspend rate 41.3% even at glucose>200
At glucose>200: SMB rate only 16.8%, suspend rate 41.3%
Optimal curve: High aggression optimal at glucose<190, low/mid optimal above — BUT confounded by glucose trajectory
Over-correction rule: IF glucose < 185 AND falling > 1.5 mg/dL/5min → reduce aggression
Top reason for no SMB at >250: manual_bolus_given (68.9%)
Hypo rule: Dosing at glucose<120 & falling>2: hypo rate 0.273 vs not-dosing 0.390 (LOWER by 0.117)
Hyper treatment rule saves -10 min TTR with -0.033 additional hypo risk
